title: OMV Petrom Starts Construction of Sustainable Fuels Unit at Petrobrazi Refinery
content: OMV Petrom has started building a Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and Renewable Diesel (HVO) unit at its Petrobrazi refinery, making it the first major sustainable fuel producer in Southeast Europe. The unit, set to be operational by 2028, will have a capacity of 250,000 tons per year and is part of a â‚¬750 million investment, which also includes two green hydrogen facilities. CEO Christina Verchere emphasized the projectâ€™s role in OMV Petromâ€™s Strategy 2030 and the energy transition, while Radu CÄƒprÄƒu highlighted the refineryâ€™s transformation to produce low-carbon fuels. The unit will process used vegetable oils and animal fats into SAF and HVO, cutting COâ‚‚ emissions by at least 65%. OMV Petrom is also expanding its EV charging network, targeting 5,000 charging points by 2030 in Romania.
author: Analysts Account
